title: prunella vulgaris subsp. surrecta
intro: Prunella vulgaris subsp. surrecta, also known as self-heal or heal-all, is a subspecies of the self-heal plant, native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its small, blue-violet flowers and heart-shaped leaves. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which contribute to its ability to aid in wound healing and reduce fever. The health benefits of Prunella vulgaris subsp. surrecta include its potential to boost the immune system, reduce stress and anxiety, and exhibit antioxidant properties. Prunella vulgaris subsp. surrecta can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support overall health and well-being.
